key_insight: Latent representations from pretrained interatomic potentials can guide crystal structure evolution more reliably than real-space crossover by preserving favorable motifs across mismatched parents.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more robust ground-state crystal structure prediction and variable-supercell search, with strong potential for inverse design of complex oxides and superlattices.
method: Learn parent latent vectors using a pretrained universal interatomic potential.
Interpolate parent latents to define a target offspring representation.
Inverse-optimize atomic positions and lattice vectors to match the target latent, generating physically plausible offspring.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved HfO2 ground-state recovery from 20-35% to 60-95%, nearly tenfold lower search cost across 16 perovskites, and discovery of a new long-period superlattice ground state.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and transferability of the pretrained latent model, so poorly trained or domain-mismatched potentials could limit robustness.
paper_title: Latent Genetic Algorithm for Crystal Structure Prediction
